.. py:function:: dimensional(data: anndata.AnnData, key: str | None = None, *, mapping: lets_plot.plot.core.FeatureSpec | None = None, dimensions: Literal['umap', 'pca', 'tsne'] = 'umap', use_key: str | None = None, xy: tuple[int, int] | collections.abc.Sequence[int] = (1, 2), size: float = 0.8, variable_keys: collections.abc.Sequence[str] | str | None = None, tooltips: Literal['none'] | collections.abc.Sequence[str] | lets_plot.plot.core.FeatureSpec | None = None, interactive: bool = False, observations_name: str = 'Barcode', color_low: str = '#e6e6e6', color_mid: str | None = None, color_high: str = '#377eb8', mid_point: Literal['mean', 'median', 'mid'] | float = 'median', axis_type: Literal['axis', 'arrow'] | None = None, arrow_length: float = 0.25, arrow_size: float = 1, arrow_color: str = '#3f3f3f', arrow_angle: float = 10, legend_ondata: bool = False, ondata_size: float = 12, ondata_color: str = '#3f3f3f', ondata_fontface: str = 'bold', ondata_family: str = 'sans', ondata_alpha: float = 1, ondata_weighted: bool = True, **point_kwargs) -> lets_plot.plot.core.PlotSpec

   Dimensionality reduction plot.

   :param data: The AnnData object of the single cell data.
   :type data: AnnData
   :param key: The key (cell feature) to color the points by.
               e.g., 'leiden' or 'louvain' to color by clusters or gene name for expression.
   :type key: str, default=None
   :param mapping: Additional aesthetic mappings for the plot, the result of `aes()`.
   :type mapping: FeatureSpec | None, default=None
   :param dimensions: The dimensional reduction method to use.
                      e.g., 'umap' or 'pca' or 'tsne'.
   :type dimensions: Literal['umap', 'pca', 'tsne'], default='umap'
   :param xy: The x and y axes to use for the plot.
              e.g., (1, 2) for UMAP1 and UMAP2.
   :type xy: tuple[int, int] | Sequence[int], default=(1, 2)
   :param use_key: The specific key to use for the desired dimensions.
                   e.g., 'X_umap_2d' or 'X_pca_2d'.
                   Otherwise, the function will decide on the key based on the dimensions.
   :type use_key: str, default=None
   :param size: The size of the points.
   :type size: float, default=0.8
   :param variable_keys: Variable keys to add to the DataFrame. If None, no additional keys are added.
   :type variable_keys: str | Sequence[str] | None, default=None
   :param tooltips: Tooltips to show when hovering over the geom.
                    Accepts Sequence[str] or result of `layer_tooltips()` for more complex tooltips.
                    Use 'none' to disable tooltips.
   :type tooltips: Literal['none'] | Sequence[str] | FeatureSpec | None, default=None
   :param interactive: Whether to make the plot interactive.
   :type interactive: bool, default=False
   :param observations_name: The name to give to barcode (or index) column in the dataframe.
   :type observations_name: str, default='Barcode'
   :param color_low: The color to use for the low end of the color gradient.
                     - Accepts:
                         - hex code e.g. '#f1f1f1'
                         - color name (of a limited set of colors).
                         - RGB/RGBA e.g. 'rgb(0, 0, 255)', 'rgba(0, 0, 255, 0.5)'.
                     - Applies to continuous (non-categorical) data.
   :type color_low: str, default='#e6e6e6'
   :param color_mid: The color to use for the middle part of the color gradient.
                     - Accepts:
                         - hex code e.g. '#f1f1f1'
                         - color name (of a limited set of colors).
                         - RGB/RGBA e.g. 'rgb(0, 0, 255)', 'rgba(0, 0, 255, 0.5)'.
                     - Applies to continuous (non-categorical) data.
   :type color_mid: str, default=None
   :param color_high: The color to use for the high end of the color gradient.
                      - Accepts:
                          - hex code e.g. '#f1f1f1'
                          - color name (of a limited set of colors).
                          - RGB/RGBA e.g. 'rgb(0, 0, 255)', 'rgba(0, 0, 255, 0.5)'.
                      - Applies to continuous (non-categorical) data.
   :type color_high: str, default='#377EB8'
   :param mid_point: The midpoint (in data value) of the color gradient.
                     Can be 'mean', 'median' and 'mid' or a number (float or int).
                     - If 'mean', the midpoint is the mean of the data.
                     - If 'median', the midpoint is the median of the data.
                     - If 'mid', the midpoint is the mean of 'min' and 'max' of the data.
   :type mid_point: Literal["mean", "median", "mid"] | float, default="median"
   :param axis_type: Whether to use regular axis or arrows as the axis.
   :type axis_type: Literal["axis", "arrow"] | None
   :param arrow_length: Length of the arrow head (px).
   :type arrow_length: float, default=0.25
   :param arrow_size: Size of the arrow.
   :type arrow_size: float, default=1
   :param arrow_color: Color of the arrows.
                       - Accepts:
                           - hex code e.g. '#f1f1f1'
                           - color name (of a limited set of colors).
                           - RGB/RGBA e.g. 'rgb(0, 0, 255)', 'rgba(0, 0, 255, 0.5)'.
                       - Applies to continuous (non-categorical) data.
   :type arrow_color: str, default='#3f3f3f'
   :param arrow_angle: Angle of the arrow head in degrees.
   :type arrow_angle: float, default=10
   :param legend_ondata: whether to show legend on data
   :type legend_ondata: bool, default=False
   :param ondata_size: size of the legend (text) on data.
   :type ondata_size: float, default=12
   :param ondata_color: color of the legend (text) on data
   :type ondata_color: str, default='#3f3f3f'
   :param ondata_fontface: fontface of the legend (text) on data.
                           https://lets-plot.org/python/pages/aesthetics.html#font-face
   :type ondata_fontface: str, default='bold'
   :param ondata_family: family of the legend (text) on data.
                         https://lets-plot.org/python/pages/aesthetics.html#font-family
   :type ondata_family: str, default='sans'
   :param ondata_alpha: alpha (transparency) of the legend on data.
   :type ondata_alpha: float, default=1
   :param ondata_weighted: whether to use weighted mean for the legend on data.
                           If True, the weighted mean of the group means is used.
                           If False, the arithmetic mean of the group means is used.
   :type ondata_weighted: bool, default=True
   :param \*\*point_kwargs: Additional parameters for the `geom_point` layer.
                            For more information on geom_point parameters, see:
                            https://lets-plot.org/python/pages/api/lets_plot.geom_point.html

   :returns: Dimensional reduction plot.
   :rtype: PlotSpec


.. py:function:: boxplot(data: anndata.AnnData, key: str | collections.abc.Sequence[str], *, mapping: lets_plot.plot.core.FeatureSpec | None = None, axis: Literal[0, 1] | None = None, color: str | None = None, fill: str | None = None, add_keys: collections.abc.Sequence[str] | str | None = None, tooltips: Literal['none'] | collections.abc.Sequence[str] | lets_plot.plot.core.FeatureSpec | None = None, geom_fill: str | None = '#FF00FF', geom_color: str | None = '#2f2f2f', point_color: str = '#1f1f1f', point_alpha: float = 0.7, point_size: float = 0.5, point_geom: Literal['jitter', 'point', 'sina'] = 'jitter', observations_name: str = 'Barcode', variables_name: str = 'Variable', show_points: bool = True, interactive: bool = False, value_column: str = 'value', variable_column: str = 'variable', point_kwargs: dict[str, Any] | None = None, **geom_kwargs) -> lets_plot.plot.core.PlotSpec

   Boxplot.

   :param data: The AnnData object of the single cell data.
   :type data: AnnData
   :param key: The key(s) to get the values (numerical).
               e.g., 'total_counts' or a gene name.
   :type key: str | Sequence[str]
   :param mapping: Additional aesthetic mappings for the plot, the result of `aes()`.
   :type mapping: FeatureSpec | None, default=None
   :param axis: axis of the data, 0 for observations and 1 for variables.
   :type axis: Literal[0,1] | None, default=None
   :param color: Color aesthetic to split the boxplot (categorical).
                 e,g., 'cell_type' or 'leiden'.
   :type color: str | None, default=None
   :param fill: Fill aesthetic to split the boxplot (categorical).
                e,g., 'cell_type' or 'leiden'.
   :type fill: str | None, default=None
   :param add_keys: Additional keys to include in the dataframe.
   :type add_keys: Sequence[str] | str | None, default=None
   :param tooltips: Tooltips to show when hovering over the geom.
                    Accepts Sequence[str] or result of `layer_tooltips()` for more complex tooltips.
                    Use 'none' to disable tooltips.
   :type tooltips: Literal['none'] | Sequence[str] | FeatureSpec | None, default=None
   :param geom_fill: Fill color for all boxplots in the boxplot.
                     - Accepts:
                         - hex code e.g. '#f1f1f1'
                         - color name (of a limited set of colors).
                         - RGB/RGBA e.g. 'rgb(0, 0, 255)', 'rgba(0, 0, 255, 0.5)'.
   :type geom_fill: str | None, default="#FF00FF"
   :param geom_color: Border color for all boxplots in the boxplot.
                      - Accepts:
                          - hex code e.g. '#f1f1f1'
                          - color name (of a limited set of colors).
                          - RGB/RGBA e.g. 'rgb(0, 0, 255)', 'rgba(0, 0, 255, 0.5)'.
   :type geom_color: str | None, default="#2f2f2f"
   :param point_color: Color for the points in the boxplot.
                       - Accepts:
                           - hex code e.g. '#f1f1f1'
                           - color name (of a limited set of colors).
                           - RGB/RGBA e.g. 'rgb(0, 0, 255)', 'rgba(0, 0, 255, 0.5)'.
   :type point_color: str, default="#1f1f1f"
   :param point_alpha: Alpha (transparency) for the points in the boxplot.
   :type point_alpha: float, default=0.7
   :param point_size: Size for the points in the boxplot.
   :type point_size: float, default=0.5
   :param point_geom: Geom type of the points, default is geom_jitter.
   :type point_geom: Literal["jitter","point","sina"], default is "jitter",
   :param observations_name: The name to give to barcode (or index) column in the dataframe.
   :type observations_name: str, default="Barcode"
   :param variables_name: The name to give to variable index column in the dataframe.
   :type variables_name: str, default="Variable"
   :param show_points: Whether to show points.
   :type show_points: bool, default=True
   :param interactive: Whether to make the plot interactive.
   :type interactive: bool, default=False
   :param variable_column: The name of the variable column in the dataframe.
   :type variable_column: str, default="variable"
   :param value_column: The name of the value column in the dataframe.
   :type value_column: str, default="value"
   :param point_kwargs: Additional parameters for the `geom_point` layer.
                        For more information on geom_point parameters, see:
                        https://lets-plot.org/python/pages/api/lets_plot.geom_point.html
   :type point_kwargs: dict[str, Any] | None, default=None
   :param \*\*geom_kwargs: Additional parameters for the `geom_boxplot` layer.
                           For more information on geom_boxplot parameters, see:
                           https://lets-plot.org/python/pages/api/lets_plot.geom_boxplot.html

   :returns: Boxplot.
   :rtype: PlotSpec


.. py:function:: violin(data: anndata.AnnData, key: str | collections.abc.Sequence[str], *, mapping: lets_plot.plot.core.FeatureSpec | None = None, axis: Literal[0, 1] | None = None, color: str | None = None, fill: str | None = None, add_keys: collections.abc.Sequence[str] | str | None = None, tooltips: Literal['none'] | collections.abc.Sequence[str] | lets_plot.plot.core.FeatureSpec | None = None, geom_fill: str | None = '#FF00FF', geom_color: str | None = '#2f2f2f', point_color: str = '#1f1f1f', point_alpha: float = 0.7, point_size: float = 0.5, point_geom: Literal['jitter', 'point', 'sina'] = 'jitter', observations_name: str = 'Barcode', variables_name: str = 'Variable', show_points: bool = True, interactive: bool = False, value_column: str = 'value', variable_column: str = 'variable', point_kwargs: dict[str, Any] | None = None, **geom_kwargs) -> lets_plot.plot.core.PlotSpec

   Violin Plot.

   :param data: The AnnData object of the single cell data.
   :type data: AnnData
   :param key: The key(s) to get the values (numerical).
               e.g., 'total_counts' or a gene name.
   :type key: str | Sequence[str]
   :param mapping: Additional aesthetic mappings for the plot, the result of `aes()`.
   :type mapping: FeatureSpec | None, default=None
   :param axis: axis of the data, 0 for observations and 1 for variables.
   :type axis: Literal[0,1] | None, default=None
   :param color: Color aesthetic to split the violin plot (categorical).
                 e,g., 'cell_type' or 'leiden'.
   :type color: str | None, default=None
   :param fill: Fill aesthetic to split the violin plot (categorical).
                e,g., 'cell_type' or 'leiden'.
   :type fill: str | None, default=None
   :param add_keys: Additional keys to include in the dataframe.
   :type add_keys: Sequence[str] | str | None, default=None
   :param tooltips: Tooltips to show when hovering over the geom.
                    Accepts Sequence[str] or result of `layer_tooltips()` for more complex tooltips.
                    Use 'none' to disable tooltips.
   :type tooltips: Literal['none'] | Sequence[str] | FeatureSpec | None, default=None
   :param geom_fill: Fill color for all violins in the violin plot.
                     - Accepts:
                         - hex code e.g. '#f1f1f1'
                         - color name (of a limited set of colors).
                         - RGB/RGBA e.g. 'rgb(0, 0, 255)', 'rgba(0, 0, 255, 0.5)'.
   :type geom_fill: str | None, default="#FF00FF"
   :param geom_color: Border color for all violins in the violin plot.
                      - Accepts:
                          - hex code e.g. '#f1f1f1'
                          - color name (of a limited set of colors).
                          - RGB/RGBA e.g. 'rgb(0, 0, 255)', 'rgba(0, 0, 255, 0.5)'.
   :type geom_color: str | None, default="#2f2f2f"
   :param point_color: Color for the points in the violin plot.
                       - Accepts:
                           - hex code e.g. '#f1f1f1'
                           - color name (of a limited set of colors).
                           - RGB/RGBA e.g. 'rgb(0, 0, 255)', 'rgba(0, 0, 255, 0.5)'.
   :type point_color: str, default="#1f1f1f"
   :param point_alpha: Alpha (transparency) for the points in the violin plot.
   :type point_alpha: float, default=0.7
   :param point_size: Size for the points in the violin plot.
   :type point_size: float, default=0.5
   :param point_geom: Geom type of the points, default is geom_jitter.
   :type point_geom: Literal["jitter","point","sina"], default is "jitter",
   :param observations_name: The name to give to barcode (or index) column in the dataframe.
   :type observations_name: str, default="Barcode"
   :param variables_name: The name to give to variable index column in the dataframe.
   :type variables_name: str, default="Variable"
   :param show_points: Whether to show points.
   :type show_points: bool, default=True
   :param interactive: Whether to make the plot interactive.
   :type interactive: bool, default=False
   :param variable_column: The name of the variable column in the dataframe.
   :type variable_column: str, default="variable"
   :param value_column: The name of the value column in the dataframe.
   :type value_column: str, default="value"
   :param point_kwargs: Additional parameters for the `geom_point` layer.
                        For more information on geom_point parameters, see:
                        https://lets-plot.org/python/pages/api/lets_plot.geom_point.html
   :type point_kwargs: dict[str, Any] | None, default=None
   :param \*\*geom_kwargs: Additional parameters for the `geom_violin` layer.
                           For more information on geom_violin parameters, see:
                           https://lets-plot.org/python/pages/api/lets_plot.geom_violin.html

   :returns: Violin plot.
   :rtype: PlotSpec


.. py:function:: boxplots(data: anndata.AnnData, keys: collections.abc.Sequence[str], *, mapping: lets_plot.plot.core.FeatureSpec | None = None, axis: Literal[0, 1] | None = None, color: str | None = None, fill: str | None = None, add_keys: collections.abc.Sequence[str] | str | None = None, tooltips: Literal['none'] | collections.abc.Sequence[str] | lets_plot.plot.core.FeatureSpec | None = None, geom_fill: str = '#FF00FF', geom_color: str = '#2f2f2f', point_color: str = '#1f1f1f', point_alpha: float = 0.7, point_size: float = 0.5, point_geom: Literal['jitter', 'point', 'sina'] = 'jitter', observations_name: str = 'Barcode', variables_name: str = 'Variable', show_points: bool = True, interactive: bool = False, value_column: str = 'value', variable_column: str = 'variable', share_axis: bool = False, share_ticks: bool = False, layers: collections.abc.Sequence[lets_plot.plot.core.FeatureSpec | lets_plot.plot.core.LayerSpec] | lets_plot.plot.core.FeatureSpec | lets_plot.plot.core.LayerSpec | None = None, ncol: int | None = None, sharex: str | None = None, sharey: str | None = None, widths: list | None = None, heights: list | None = None, hspace: float | None = None, vspace: float | None = None, fit: bool | None = None, align: bool | None = None, guides: str = 'auto', point_kwargs: dict[str, Any] | None = None, **geom_kwargs) -> lets_plot.plot.subplots.SupPlotsSpec

   Boxplots.

   :param data: The AnnData object of the single cell data.
   :type data: AnnData
   :param keys: The keys to get the values (numerical).
                e.g., ['total_counts', 'pct_counts_in_top_50_genes'] or a list of gene names.
   :type keys: list[str] | tuple[str] | Sequence[str]
   :param mapping: Additional aesthetic mappings for the plot, the result of `aes()`.
   :type mapping: FeatureSpec | None, default=None
   :param axis: axis of the data, 0 for observations and 1 for variables.
   :type axis: Literal[0,1] | None, default=None
   :param color: Color aesthetic to split the boxplot (categorical).
                 e,g., 'cell_type' or 'leiden'.
   :type color: str | None, default=None
   :param fill: Fill aesthetic to split the boxplot (categorical).
                e,g., 'cell_type' or 'leiden'.
   :type fill: str | None, default=None
   :param add_keys: Additional keys to include in the dataframe.
   :type add_keys: Sequence[str] | str | None, default=None
   :param tooltips: Tooltips to show when hovering over the geom.
                    Accepts Sequence[str] or result of `layer_tooltips()` for more complex tooltips.
                    Use 'none' to disable tooltips.
   :type tooltips: Literal['none'] | Sequence[str] | FeatureSpec | None, default=None
   :param geom_fill: Fill color for all boxplots in the boxplot.
                     - Accepts:
                         - hex code e.g. '#f1f1f1'
                         - color name (of a limited set of colors).
                         - RGB/RGBA e.g. 'rgb(0, 0, 255)', 'rgba(0, 0, 255, 0.5)'.
   :type geom_fill: str, default="#FF00FF"
   :param geom_color: Border color for all boxplots in the boxplot.
                      - Accepts:
                          - hex code e.g. '#f1f1f1'
                          - color name (of a limited set of colors).
                          - RGB/RGBA e.g. 'rgb(0, 0, 255)', 'rgba(0, 0, 255, 0.5)'.
   :type geom_color: str, default="#2f2f2f"
   :param point_color: Color for the points in the boxplot.
                       - Accepts:
                           - hex code e.g. '#f1f1f1'
                           - color name (of a limited set of colors).
                           - RGB/RGBA e.g. 'rgb(0, 0, 255)', 'rgba(0, 0, 255, 0.5)'.
   :type point_color: str, default="#1f1f1f"
   :param point_alpha: Alpha (transparency) for the points in the boxplot.
   :type point_alpha: float, default=0.7
   :param point_size: Size for the points in the boxplot.
   :type point_size: float, default=0.5
   :param point_geom: Geom type of the points, default is geom_jitter.
   :type point_geom: Literal["jitter","point","sina"], default is "jitter",
   :param observations_name: The name to give to barcode (or index) column in the dataframe.
   :type observations_name: str, default="Barcode"
   :param variables_name: The name to give to variable index column in the dataframe.
   :type variables_name: str, default="Gene"
   :param show_points: Whether to show points.
   :type show_points: bool, default=True
   :param interactive: Whether to make the plot interactive.
   :type interactive: bool, default=False
   :param share_ticks: Whether to share the labels across all plots.
                       If True, only X tick texts on bottom row and Y tick text on left column are shown.
   :type share_ticks: bool, default=True
   :param share_axis: Whether to share the axis across all plots.
                      If True, only X axis on bottom row and Y axis on left column is shown.
   :type share_axis: bool, default=False
   :param layers: Additional layers to add to the plot.
   :type layers: Sequence[FeatureSpec | LayerSpec] | FeatureSpec | LayerSpec | None, default=None
   :param variable_column: The name of the variable column in the dataframe.
   :type variable_column: str, default="variable"
   :param value_column: The name of the value column in the dataframe.
   :type value_column: str, default="value"
   :param ncol: Number of columns in grid. If not specified, shows plots horizontally, in one row.
   :type ncol: int, default=None
   :param sharex: Controls sharing of axis limits between subplots in the grid.
                  `all`/True - share limits between all subplots.
                  `none`/False - do not share limits between subplots.
                  `row` - share limits between subplots in the same row.
                  `col` - share limits between subplots in the same column.
   :type sharex: bool, default=None
   :param sharey: Controls sharing of axis limits between subplots in the grid.
                  `all`/True - share limits between all subplots.
                  `none`/False - do not share limits between subplots.
                  `row` - share limits between subplots in the same row.
                  `col` - share limits between subplots in the same column.
   :type sharey: bool, default=None
   :param widths: Relative width of each column of grid, left to right.
   :type widths: list[float], default=None
   :param heights: Relative height of each row of grid, top-down.
   :type heights: list[float], default=None
   :param hspace: Cell horizontal spacing in px.
   :type hspace: float | None = None
   :param vspace: Cell vertical spacing in px.
   :type vspace: float | None = None
   :param fit: Whether to stretch each plot to match the aspect ratio of its cell (fit=True),
               or to preserve the original aspect ratio of plots (fit=False).
   :type fit: bool, default=True
   :param align: If True, align inner areas (i.e. “geom” bounds) of plots.
                 However, cells containing other (sub)grids are not participating
                 in the plot “inner areas” layouting.
   :type align: bool, default=False
   :param guides: Specifies how guides (legends and colorbars) should be treated in the layout.
                  - 'collect'- collect guides from all subplots, removing duplicates.
                  - 'keep' - keep guides in their original subplots; do not collect at this level.
                  - 'auto' - allow guides to be collected if an upper-level layout uses guides='collect';
                  otherwise, keep them in subplots.

                  Duplicates are identified by comparing visual properties:
                  For legends: title, labels, and all aesthetic values (colors, shapes, sizes, etc.).
                  For colorbars: title, domain limits, breaks, and color gradient.
   :type guides: str, default="auto"
   :param For more information on gggrid parameters:
   :param https:
   :type https: //lets-plot.org/python/pages/api/lets_plot.gggrid.html
   :param point_kwargs: Additional parameters for the `geom_point` layer.
                        For more information on geom_point parameters, see:
                        https://lets-plot.org/python/pages/api/lets_plot.geom_point.html
   :type point_kwargs: dict[str, Any] | None, default=None
   :param \*\*geom_kwargs: Additional parameters for the `geom_boxplot` layer.
                           For more information on geom_boxplot parameters, see:
                           https://lets-plot.org/python/pages/api/lets_plot.geom_boxplot.html

   :returns: Boxplots.
   :rtype: SupPlotsSpec


.. py:function:: violins(data: anndata.AnnData, keys: collections.abc.Sequence[str], *, mapping: lets_plot.plot.core.FeatureSpec | None = None, axis: Literal[0, 1] | None = None, color: str | None = None, fill: str | None = None, add_keys: collections.abc.Sequence[str] | str | None = None, tooltips: Literal['none'] | collections.abc.Sequence[str] | lets_plot.plot.core.FeatureSpec | None = None, geom_fill: str = '#FF00FF', geom_color: str = '#2f2f2f', point_color: str = '#1f1f1f', point_alpha: float = 0.7, point_size: float = 0.5, point_geom: Literal['jitter', 'point', 'sina'] = 'jitter', observations_name: str = 'Barcode', variables_name: str = 'Variable', show_points: bool = True, interactive: bool = False, value_column: str = 'value', variable_column: str = 'variable', share_axis: bool = False, share_ticks: bool = False, layers: collections.abc.Sequence[lets_plot.plot.core.FeatureSpec | lets_plot.plot.core.LayerSpec] | lets_plot.plot.core.FeatureSpec | lets_plot.plot.core.LayerSpec | None = None, ncol: int | None = None, sharex: str | None = None, sharey: str | None = None, widths: list | None = None, heights: list | None = None, hspace: float | None = None, vspace: float | None = None, fit: bool | None = None, align: bool | None = None, guides: str = 'auto', point_kwargs: dict[str, Any] | None = None, **geom_kwargs) -> lets_plot.plot.subplots.SupPlotsSpec

   Violin Plots.

   :param data: The AnnData object of the single cell data.
   :type data: AnnData
   :param keys: The keys to get the values (numerical).
                e.g., ['total_counts', 'pct_counts_in_top_50_genes'] or a list of gene names.
   :type keys: list[str] | tuple[str] | Sequence[str]
   :param mapping: Additional aesthetic mappings for the plot, the result of `aes()`.
   :type mapping: FeatureSpec | None, default=None
   :param axis: axis of the data, 0 for observations and 1 for variables.
   :type axis: Literal[0,1] | None, default=None
   :param color: Color aesthetic to split the violin plot (categorical).
                 e,g., 'cell_type' or 'leiden'.
   :type color: str | None, default=None
   :param fill: Fill aesthetic to split the violin plot (categorical).
                e,g., 'cell_type' or 'leiden'.
   :type fill: str | None, default=None
   :param add_keys: Additional keys to include in the dataframe.
   :type add_keys: Sequence[str] | str | None, default=None
   :param tooltips: Tooltips to show when hovering over the geom.
                    Accepts Sequence[str] or result of `layer_tooltips()` for more complex tooltips.
                    Use 'none' to disable tooltips.
   :type tooltips: Literal['none'] | Sequence[str] | FeatureSpec | None, default=None
   :param geom_fill: Fill color for all violins in the violin plot.
                     - Accepts:
                         - hex code e.g. '#f1f1f1'
                         - color name (of a limited set of colors).
                         - RGB/RGBA e.g. 'rgb(0, 0, 255)', 'rgba(0, 0, 255, 0.5)'.
   :type geom_fill: str, default="#FF00FF"
   :param geom_color: Border color for all violins in the violin plot.
                      - Accepts:
                          - hex code e.g. '#f1f1f1'
                          - color name (of a limited set of colors).
                          - RGB/RGBA e.g. 'rgb(0, 0, 255)', 'rgba(0, 0, 255, 0.5)'.
   :type geom_color: str, default="#2f2f2f"
   :param point_color: Color for the points in the violin plot.
                       - Accepts:
                           - hex code e.g. '#f1f1f1'
                           - color name (of a limited set of colors).
                           - RGB/RGBA e.g. 'rgb(0, 0, 255)', 'rgba(0, 0, 255, 0.5)'.
   :type point_color: str, default="#1f1f1f"
   :param point_alpha: Alpha (transparency) for the points in the violin plot.
   :type point_alpha: float, default=0.7
   :param point_size: Size for the points in the violin plot.
   :type point_size: float, default=0.5
   :param point_geom: Geom type of the points, default is geom_jitter.
   :type point_geom: Literal["jitter","point","sina"], default is "jitter",
   :param trim: Whether to trim the violin plot.
   :type trim: bool, default=False
   :param observations_name: The name to give to barcode (or index) column in the dataframe.
   :type observations_name: str, default="Barcode"
   :param variables_name: The name to give to variable index column in the dataframe.
   :type variables_name: str, default="Gene"
   :param show_points: Whether to show points.
   :type show_points: bool, default=True
   :param interactive: Whether to make the plot interactive.
   :type interactive: bool, default=False
   :param share_ticks: Whether to share the labels across all plots.
                       If True, only X tick texts on bottom row and Y tick text on left column are shown.
   :type share_ticks: bool, default=True
   :param share_axis: Whether to share the axis across all plots.
                      If True, only X axis on bottom row and Y axis on left column is shown.
   :type share_axis: bool, default=False
   :param layers: Additional layers to add to the plot.
   :type layers: Sequence[FeatureSpec | LayerSpec] | FeatureSpec | LayerSpec | None, default=None
   :param variable_column: The name of the variable column in the dataframe.
   :type variable_column: str, default="variable"
   :param value_column: The name of the value column in the dataframe.
   :type value_column: str, default="value"
   :param ncol: Number of columns in grid. If not specified, shows plots horizontally, in one row.
   :type ncol: int, default=None
   :param sharex: Controls sharing of axis limits between subplots in the grid.
                  `all`/True - share limits between all subplots.
                  `none`/False - do not share limits between subplots.
                  `row` - share limits between subplots in the same row.
                  `col` - share limits between subplots in the same column.
   :type sharex: bool, default=None
   :param sharey: Controls sharing of axis limits between subplots in the grid.
                  `all`/True - share limits between all subplots.
                  `none`/False - do not share limits between subplots.
                  `row` - share limits between subplots in the same row.
                  `col` - share limits between subplots in the same column.
   :type sharey: bool, default=None
   :param widths: Relative width of each column of grid, left to right.
   :type widths: list[float], default=None
   :param heights: Relative height of each row of grid, top-down.
   :type heights: list[float], default=None
   :param hspace: Cell horizontal spacing in px.
   :type hspace: float | None = None
   :param vspace: Cell vertical spacing in px.
   :type vspace: float | None = None
   :param fit: Whether to stretch each plot to match the aspect ratio of its cell (fit=True),
               or to preserve the original aspect ratio of plots (fit=False).
   :type fit: bool, default=True
   :param align: If True, align inner areas (i.e. “geom” bounds) of plots.
                 However, cells containing other (sub)grids are not participating
                 in the plot “inner areas” layouting.
   :type align: bool, default=False
   :param guides: Specifies how guides (legends and colorbars) should be treated in the layout.
                  - 'collect'- collect guides from all subplots, removing duplicates.
                  - 'keep' - keep guides in their original subplots; do not collect at this level.
                  - 'auto' - allow guides to be collected if an upper-level layout uses guides='collect';
                  otherwise, keep them in subplots.

                  Duplicates are identified by comparing visual properties:
                  For legends: title, labels, and all aesthetic values (colors, shapes, sizes, etc.).
                  For colorbars: title, domain limits, breaks, and color gradient.
   :type guides: str, default="auto"
   :param For more information on gggrid parameters:
   :param https:
   :type https: //lets-plot.org/python/pages/api/lets_plot.gggrid.html
   :param point_kwargs: Additional parameters for the `geom_point` layer.
                        For more information on geom_point parameters, see:
                        https://lets-plot.org/python/pages/api/lets_plot.geom_point.html
   :type point_kwargs: dict[str, Any] | None, default=None
   :param \*\*geom_kwargs: Additional parameters for the `geom_violin` layer.
                           For more information on geom_violin parameters, see:
                           https://lets-plot.org/python/pages/api/lets_plot.geom_violin.html

   :returns: Violin Plots.
   :rtype: SupPlotsSpec


.. py:function:: expression(data: anndata.AnnData, key: str, *, mapping: lets_plot.plot.core.FeatureSpec | None = None, dimensions: Literal['umap', 'pca', 'tsne'] = 'umap', use_key: str | None = None, xy: tuple[int, int] | collections.abc.Sequence[int] = (1, 2), size: float = 0.8, variable_keys: collections.abc.Sequence[str] | str | None = None, tooltips: Literal['none'] | collections.abc.Sequence[str] | lets_plot.plot.core.FeatureSpec | None = None, interactive: bool = False, observations_name: str = 'Barcode', color_low: str = '#e6e6e6', color_mid: str | None = None, color_high: str = '#377eb8', mid_point: Literal['mean', 'median', 'mid'] | float = 'median', axis_type: Literal['axis', 'arrow'] | None = 'arrow', arrow_length: float = 0.25, arrow_size: float = 1, arrow_color: str = '#3f3f3f', arrow_angle: float = 10, legend_ondata: bool = False, ondata_size: float = 12, ondata_color: str = '#3f3f3f', ondata_fontface: str = 'bold', ondata_family: str = 'sans', ondata_alpha: float = 1, ondata_weighted: bool = True, **point_kwargs) -> lets_plot.plot.core.PlotSpec

   Dimensionality reduction plot of expression data.

   :param data: The AnnData object of the single cell data.
   :type data: AnnData
   :param key: The key (gene names) to color the points by.
   :type key: str
   :param mapping: Additional aesthetic mappings for the plot, the result of `aes()`.
   :type mapping: FeatureSpec | None, default=None
   :param dimensions: The dimensional reduction method to use.
                      e.g., 'umap' or 'pca' or 'tsne'.
   :type dimensions: Literal['umap', 'pca', 'tsne'], default='umap'
   :param use_key: The specific key to use for the desired dimensions.
                   e.g., 'X_umap_2d' or 'X_pca_2d'.
                   Otherwise, the function will decide on the key based on the dimensions.
   :type use_key: str, default=None
   :param xy: The x and y axes to use for the plot.
              e.g., (1, 2) for UMAP1 and UMAP2.
   :type xy: tuple[int, int] | Sequence[int], default=(1, 2)
   :param size: The size of the points.
   :type size: float, default=0.8
   :param variable_keys: Variable keys to add to the DataFrame. If None, no additional keys are added.
   :type variable_keys: str | Sequence[str] | None, default=None
   :param tooltips: Tooltips to show when hovering over the geom.
                    Accepts Sequence[str] or result of `layer_tooltips()` for more complex tooltips.
                    Use 'none' to disable tooltips.
   :type tooltips: Literal['none'] | Sequence[str] | FeatureSpec | None, default=None
   :param interactive: Whether to make the plot interactive.
   :type interactive: bool, default=False
   :param observations_name: The name to give to barcode (or index) column in the dataframe.
   :type observations_name: str, default='Barcode'
   :param color_low: The color to use for the low end of the color gradient.
                     - Accepts:
                         - hex code e.g. '#f1f1f1'
                         - color name (of a limited set of colors).
                         - RGB/RGBA e.g. 'rgb(0, 0, 255)', 'rgba(0, 0, 255, 0.5)'.
                     - Applies to continuous (non-categorical) data.
   :type color_low: str, default='#e6e6e6'
   :param color_mid: The color to use for the middle part of the color gradient.
                     - Accepts:
                         - hex code e.g. '#f1f1f1'
                         - color name (of a limited set of colors).
                         - RGB/RGBA e.g. 'rgb(0, 0, 255)', 'rgba(0, 0, 255, 0.5)'.
                     - Applies to continuous (non-categorical) data.
   :type color_mid: str, default=None
   :param color_high: The color to use for the high end of the color gradient.
                      - Accepts:
                          - hex code e.g. '#f1f1f1'
                          - color name (of a limited set of colors).
                          - RGB/RGBA e.g. 'rgb(0, 0, 255)', 'rgba(0, 0, 255, 0.5)'.
                      - Applies to continuous (non-categorical) data.
   :type color_high: str, default='#377EB8'
   :param mid_point: The midpoint (in data value) of the color gradient.
                     Can be 'mean', 'median' and 'mid' or a number (float or int).
                     - If 'mean', the midpoint is the mean of the data.
                     - If 'median', the midpoint is the median of the data.
                     - If 'mid', the midpoint is the mean of 'min' and 'max' of the data.
   :type mid_point: Literal["mean", "median", "mid"] | float, default="median"
   :param axis_type: Whether to use regular axis or arrows as the axis.
   :type axis_type: Literal["axis", "arrow"] | None
   :param arrow_length: Length of the arrow head (px).
   :type arrow_length: float, default=0.25
   :param arrow_size: Size of the arrow.
   :type arrow_size: float, default=1
   :param arrow_color: Color of the arrows.
                       - Accepts:
                           - hex code e.g. '#f1f1f1'
                           - color name (of a limited set of colors).
                           - RGB/RGBA e.g. 'rgb(0, 0, 255)', 'rgba(0, 0, 255, 0.5)'.
                       - Applies to continuous (non-categorical) data.
   :type arrow_color: str, default='#3f3f3f'
   :param arrow_angle: Angle of the arrow head in degrees.
   :type arrow_angle: float, default=10
   :param legend_ondata: whether to show legend on data
   :type legend_ondata: bool, default=False
   :param ondata_size: size of the legend (text) on data.
   :type ondata_size: float, default=12
   :param ondata_color: color of the legend (text) on data
   :type ondata_color: str, default='#3f3f3f'
   :param ondata_fontface: fontface of the legend (text) on data.
                           https://lets-plot.org/python/pages/aesthetics.html#font-face
   :type ondata_fontface: str, default='bold'
   :param ondata_family: family of the legend (text) on data.
                         https://lets-plot.org/python/pages/aesthetics.html#font-family
   :type ondata_family: str, default='sans'
   :param ondata_alpha: alpha (transparency) of the legend on data.
   :type ondata_alpha: float, default=1
   :param ondata_weighted: whether to use weighted mean for the legend on data.
                           If True, the weighted mean of the group means is used.
                           If False, the arithmetic mean of the group means is used.
   :type ondata_weighted: bool, default=True
   :param \*\*point_kwargs: Additional parameters for the `geom_point` layer.
                            For more information on geom_point parameters, see:
                            https://lets-plot.org/python/pages/api/lets_plot.geom_point.html

   :returns: Dimensional reduction plot.
   :rtype: PlotSpec


.. py:function:: pca(data: anndata.AnnData, key: str | None = None, *, mapping: lets_plot.plot.core.FeatureSpec | None = None, use_key: str | None = None, xy: tuple[int, int] | collections.abc.Sequence[int] = (1, 2), size: float = 0.8, variable_keys: collections.abc.Sequence[str] | str | None = None, tooltips: Literal['none'] | collections.abc.Sequence[str] | lets_plot.plot.core.FeatureSpec | None = None, interactive: bool = False, observations_name: str = 'Barcode', color_low: str = '#e6e6e6', color_mid: str | None = None, color_high: str = '#377eb8', mid_point: Literal['mean', 'median', 'mid'] | float = 'median', axis_type: Literal['axis', 'arrow'] | None = None, arrow_length: float = 0.25, arrow_size: float = 1, arrow_color: str = '#3f3f3f', arrow_angle: float = 10, legend_ondata: bool = False, ondata_size: float = 12, ondata_color: str = '#3f3f3f', ondata_fontface: str = 'bold', ondata_family: str = 'sans', ondata_alpha: float = 1, ondata_weighted: bool = True, **point_kwargs) -> lets_plot.plot.core.PlotSpec

   PCA Dimensionality reduction plot.

   :param data: The AnnData object of the single cell data.
   :type data: AnnData
   :param key: The key (cell feature) to color the points by.
               e.g., 'leiden' or 'louvain' to color by clusters or gene name for expression.
   :type key: str, default=None
   :param mapping: Additional aesthetic mappings for the plot, the result of `aes()`.
   :type mapping: FeatureSpec | None, default=None
   :param use_key: The specific key to use for the desired dimensions.
                   e.g., 'X_umap_2d' or 'X_pca_2d'.
                   Otherwise, the function will decide on the key based on the dimensions.
   :type use_key: str, default=None
   :param xy: The x and y axes to use for the plot.
              e.g., (1, 2) for UMAP1 and UMAP2.
   :type xy: tuple[int, int] | Sequence[int], default=(1, 2)
   :param size: The size of the points.
   :type size: float, default=0.8
   :param variable_keys: Variable keys to add to the DataFrame. If None, no additional keys are added.
   :type variable_keys: str | Sequence[str] | None, default=None
   :param tooltips: Tooltips to show when hovering over the geom.
                    Accepts Sequence[str] or result of `layer_tooltips()` for more complex tooltips.
                    Use 'none' to disable tooltips.
   :type tooltips: Literal['none'] | Sequence[str] | FeatureSpec | None, default=None
   :param interactive: Whether to make the plot interactive.
   :type interactive: bool, default=False
   :param observations_name: The name to give to barcode (or index) column in the dataframe.
   :type observations_name: str, default='Barcode'
   :param color_low: The color to use for the low end of the color gradient.
                     - Accepts:
                         - hex code e.g. '#f1f1f1'
                         - color name (of a limited set of colors).
                         - RGB/RGBA e.g. 'rgb(0, 0, 255)', 'rgba(0, 0, 255, 0.5)'.
                     - Applies to continuous (non-categorical) data.
   :type color_low: str, default='#e6e6e6'
   :param color_mid: The color to use for the middle part of the color gradient.
                     - Accepts:
                         - hex code e.g. '#f1f1f1'
                         - color name (of a limited set of colors).
                         - RGB/RGBA e.g. 'rgb(0, 0, 255)', 'rgba(0, 0, 255, 0.5)'.
                     - Applies to continuous (non-categorical) data.
   :type color_mid: str, default=None
   :param color_high: The color to use for the high end of the color gradient.
                      - Accepts:
                          - hex code e.g. '#f1f1f1'
                          - color name (of a limited set of colors).
                          - RGB/RGBA e.g. 'rgb(0, 0, 255)', 'rgba(0, 0, 255, 0.5)'.
                      - Applies to continuous (non-categorical) data.
   :type color_high: str, default='#377EB8'
   :param mid_point: The midpoint (in data value) of the color gradient.
                     Can be 'mean', 'median' and 'mid' or a number (float or int).
                     - If 'mean', the midpoint is the mean of the data.
                     - If 'median', the midpoint is the median of the data.
                     - If 'mid', the midpoint is the mean of 'min' and 'max' of the data.
   :type mid_point: Literal["mean", "median", "mid"] | float, default="median"
   :param axis_type: Whether to use regular axis or arrows as the axis.
   :type axis_type: Literal["axis", "arrow"] | None
   :param arrow_length: Length of the arrow head (px).
   :type arrow_length: float, default=0.25
   :param arrow_size: Size of the arrow.
   :type arrow_size: float, default=1
   :param arrow_color: Color of the arrows.
                       - Accepts:
                           - hex code e.g. '#f1f1f1'
                           - color name (of a limited set of colors).
                           - RGB/RGBA e.g. 'rgb(0, 0, 255)', 'rgba(0, 0, 255, 0.5)'.
                       - Applies to continuous (non-categorical) data.
   :type arrow_color: str, default='#3f3f3f'
   :param arrow_angle: Angle of the arrow head in degrees.
   :type arrow_angle: float, default=10
   :param legend_ondata: whether to show legend on data
   :type legend_ondata: bool, default=False
   :param ondata_size: size of the legend (text) on data.
   :type ondata_size: float, default=12
   :param ondata_color: color of the legend (text) on data
   :type ondata_color: str, default='#3f3f3f'
   :param ondata_fontface: fontface of the legend (text) on data.
                           https://lets-plot.org/python/pages/aesthetics.html#font-face
   :type ondata_fontface: str, default='bold'
   :param ondata_family: family of the legend (text) on data.
                         https://lets-plot.org/python/pages/aesthetics.html#font-family
   :type ondata_family: str, default='sans'
   :param ondata_alpha: alpha (transparency) of the legend on data.
   :type ondata_alpha: float, default=1
   :param ondata_weighted: whether to use weighted mean for the legend on data.
                           If True, the weighted mean of the group means is used.
                           If False, the arithmetic mean of the group means is used.
   :type ondata_weighted: bool, default=True
   :param \*\*point_kwargs: Additional parameters for the `geom_point` layer.
                            For more information on geom_point parameters, see:
                            https://lets-plot.org/python/pages/api/lets_plot.geom_point.html

   :returns: Dimensional reduction plot.
   :rtype: PlotSpec


.. py:function:: tsne(data: anndata.AnnData, key: str | None = None, *, mapping: lets_plot.plot.core.FeatureSpec | None = None, use_key: str | None = None, xy: tuple[int, int] | collections.abc.Sequence[int] = (1, 2), size: float = 0.8, variable_keys: collections.abc.Sequence[str] | str | None = None, tooltips: Literal['none'] | collections.abc.Sequence[str] | lets_plot.plot.core.FeatureSpec | None = None, interactive: bool = False, observations_name: str = 'Barcode', color_low: str = '#e6e6e6', color_mid: str | None = None, color_high: str = '#377eb8', mid_point: Literal['mean', 'median', 'mid'] | float = 'median', axis_type: Literal['axis', 'arrow'] | None = None, arrow_length: float = 0.25, arrow_size: float = 1, arrow_color: str = '#3f3f3f', arrow_angle: float = 10, legend_ondata: bool = False, ondata_size: float = 12, ondata_color: str = '#3f3f3f', ondata_fontface: str = 'bold', ondata_family: str = 'sans', ondata_alpha: float = 1, ondata_weighted: bool = True, **point_kwargs) -> lets_plot.plot.core.PlotSpec

   tSNE Dimensionality reduction plot.

   :param data: The AnnData object of the single cell data.
   :type data: AnnData
   :param key: The key (cell feature) to color the points by.
               e.g., 'leiden' or 'louvain' to color by clusters or gene name for expression.
   :type key: str, default=None
   :param mapping: Additional aesthetic mappings for the plot, the result of `aes()`.
   :type mapping: FeatureSpec | None, default=None
   :param use_key: The specific key to use for the desired dimensions.
                   e.g., 'X_umap_2d' or 'X_pca_2d'.
                   Otherwise, the function will decide on the key based on the dimensions.
   :type use_key: str, default=None
   :param xy: The x and y axes to use for the plot.
              e.g., (1, 2) for UMAP1 and UMAP2.
   :type xy: tuple[int, int] | Sequence[int], default=(1, 2)
   :param size: The size of the points.
   :type size: float, default=0.8
   :param variable_keys: Variable keys to add to the DataFrame. If None, no additional keys are added.
   :type variable_keys: str | Sequence[str] | None, default=None
   :param tooltips: Tooltips to show when hovering over the geom.
                    Accepts Sequence[str] or result of `layer_tooltips()` for more complex tooltips.
                    Use 'none' to disable tooltips.
   :type tooltips: Literal['none'] | Sequence[str] | FeatureSpec | None, default=None
   :param interactive: Whether to make the plot interactive.
   :type interactive: bool, default=False
   :param observations_name: The name to give to barcode (or index) column in the dataframe.
   :type observations_name: str, default='Barcode'
   :param color_low: The color to use for the low end of the color gradient.
                     - Accepts:
                         - hex code e.g. '#f1f1f1'
                         - color name (of a limited set of colors).
                         - RGB/RGBA e.g. 'rgb(0, 0, 255)', 'rgba(0, 0, 255, 0.5)'.
                     - Applies to continuous (non-categorical) data.
   :type color_low: str, default='#e6e6e6'
   :param color_mid: The color to use for the middle part of the color gradient.
                     - Accepts:
                         - hex code e.g. '#f1f1f1'
                         - color name (of a limited set of colors).
                         - RGB/RGBA e.g. 'rgb(0, 0, 255)', 'rgba(0, 0, 255, 0.5)'.
                     - Applies to continuous (non-categorical) data.
   :type color_mid: str, default=None
   :param color_high: The color to use for the high end of the color gradient.
                      - Accepts:
                          - hex code e.g. '#f1f1f1'
                          - color name (of a limited set of colors).
                          - RGB/RGBA e.g. 'rgb(0, 0, 255)', 'rgba(0, 0, 255, 0.5)'.
                      - Applies to continuous (non-categorical) data.
   :type color_high: str, default='#377EB8'
   :param mid_point: The midpoint (in data value) of the color gradient.
                     Can be 'mean', 'median' and 'mid' or a number (float or int).
                     - If 'mean', the midpoint is the mean of the data.
                     - If 'median', the midpoint is the median of the data.
                     - If 'mid', the midpoint is the mean of 'min' and 'max' of the data.
   :type mid_point: Literal["mean", "median", "mid"] | float, default="median"
   :param axis_type: Whether to use regular axis or arrows as the axis.
   :type axis_type: Literal["axis", "arrow"] | None
   :param arrow_length: Length of the arrow head (px).
   :type arrow_length: float, default=0.25
   :param arrow_size: Size of the arrow.
   :type arrow_size: float, default=1
   :param arrow_color: Color of the arrows.
                       - Accepts:
                           - hex code e.g. '#f1f1f1'
                           - color name (of a limited set of colors).
                           - RGB/RGBA e.g. 'rgb(0, 0, 255)', 'rgba(0, 0, 255, 0.5)'.
                       - Applies to continuous (non-categorical) data.
   :type arrow_color: str, default='#3f3f3f'
   :param arrow_angle: Angle of the arrow head in degrees.
   :type arrow_angle: float, default=10
   :param legend_ondata: whether to show legend on data
   :type legend_ondata: bool, default=False
   :param ondata_size: size of the legend (text) on data.
   :type ondata_size: float, default=12
   :param ondata_color: color of the legend (text) on data
   :type ondata_color: str, default='#3f3f3f'
   :param ondata_fontface: fontface of the legend (text) on data.
                           https://lets-plot.org/python/pages/aesthetics.html#font-face
   :type ondata_fontface: str, default='bold'
   :param ondata_family: family of the legend (text) on data.
                         https://lets-plot.org/python/pages/aesthetics.html#font-family
   :type ondata_family: str, default='sans'
   :param ondata_alpha: alpha (transparency) of the legend on data.
   :type ondata_alpha: float, default=1
   :param ondata_weighted: whether to use weighted mean for the legend on data.
                           If True, the weighted mean of the group means is used.
                           If False, the arithmetic mean of the group means is used.
   :type ondata_weighted: bool, default=True
   :param \*\*point_kwargs: Additional parameters for the `geom_point` layer.
                            For more information on geom_point parameters, see:
                            https://lets-plot.org/python/pages/api/lets_plot.geom_point.html

   :returns: Dimensional reduction plot.
   :rtype: PlotSpec


.. py:function:: umap(data: anndata.AnnData, key: str | None = None, *, mapping: lets_plot.plot.core.FeatureSpec | None = None, use_key: str | None = None, xy: tuple[int, int] | collections.abc.Sequence[int] = (1, 2), size: float = 0.8, variable_keys: collections.abc.Sequence[str] | str | None = None, tooltips: Literal['none'] | collections.abc.Sequence[str] | lets_plot.plot.core.FeatureSpec | None = None, interactive: bool = False, observations_name: str = 'Barcode', color_low: str = '#e6e6e6', color_mid: str | None = None, color_high: str = '#377eb8', mid_point: Literal['mean', 'median', 'mid'] | float = 'median', axis_type: Literal['axis', 'arrow'] | None = None, arrow_length: float = 0.25, arrow_size: float = 1, arrow_color: str = '#3f3f3f', arrow_angle: float = 10, legend_ondata: bool = False, ondata_size: float = 12, ondata_color: str = '#3f3f3f', ondata_fontface: str = 'bold', ondata_family: str = 'sans', ondata_alpha: float = 1, ondata_weighted: bool = True, **point_kwargs) -> lets_plot.plot.core.PlotSpec

   UMAP Dimensionality reduction plot.

   :param data: The AnnData object of the single cell data.
   :type data: AnnData
   :param key: The key (cell feature) to color the points by.
               e.g., 'leiden' or 'louvain' to color by clusters or gene name for expression.
   :type key: str, default=None
   :param mapping: Additional aesthetic mappings for the plot, the result of `aes()`.
   :type mapping: FeatureSpec | None, default=None
   :param use_key: The specific key to use for the desired dimensions.
                   e.g., 'X_umap_2d' or 'X_pca_2d'.
                   Otherwise, the function will decide on the key based on the dimensions.
   :type use_key: str, default=None
   :param xy: The x and y axes to use for the plot.
              e.g., (1, 2) for UMAP1 and UMAP2.
   :type xy: tuple[int, int] | Sequence[int], default=(1, 2)
   :param size: The size of the points.
   :type size: float, default=0.8
   :param variable_keys: Variable keys to add to the DataFrame. If None, no additional keys are added.
   :type variable_keys: str | Sequence[str] | None, default=None
   :param tooltips: Tooltips to show when hovering over the geom.
                    Accepts Sequence[str] or result of `layer_tooltips()` for more complex tooltips.
                    Use 'none' to disable tooltips.
   :type tooltips: Literal['none'] | Sequence[str] | FeatureSpec | None, default=None
   :param interactive: Whether to make the plot interactive.
   :type interactive: bool, default=False
   :param observations_name: The name to give to barcode (or index) column in the dataframe.
   :type observations_name: str, default='Barcode'
   :param color_low: The color to use for the low end of the color gradient.
                     - Accepts:
                         - hex code e.g. '#f1f1f1'
                         - color name (of a limited set of colors).
                         - RGB/RGBA e.g. 'rgb(0, 0, 255)', 'rgba(0, 0, 255, 0.5)'.
                     - Applies to continuous (non-categorical) data.
   :type color_low: str, default='#e6e6e6'
   :param color_mid: The color to use for the middle part of the color gradient.
                     - Accepts:
                         - hex code e.g. '#f1f1f1'
                         - color name (of a limited set of colors).
                         - RGB/RGBA e.g. 'rgb(0, 0, 255)', 'rgba(0, 0, 255, 0.5)'.
                     - Applies to continuous (non-categorical) data.
   :type color_mid: str, default=None
   :param color_high: The color to use for the high end of the color gradient.
                      - Accepts:
                          - hex code e.g. '#f1f1f1'
                          - color name (of a limited set of colors).
                          - RGB/RGBA e.g. 'rgb(0, 0, 255)', 'rgba(0, 0, 255, 0.5)'.
                      - Applies to continuous (non-categorical) data.
   :type color_high: str, default='#377EB8'
   :param mid_point: The midpoint (in data value) of the color gradient.
                     Can be 'mean', 'median' and 'mid' or a number (float or int).
                     - If 'mean', the midpoint is the mean of the data.
                     - If 'median', the midpoint is the median of the data.
                     - If 'mid', the midpoint is the mean of 'min' and 'max' of the data.
   :type mid_point: Literal["mean", "median", "mid"] | float, default="median"
   :param axis_type: Whether to use regular axis or arrows as the axis.
   :type axis_type: Literal["axis", "arrow"] | None
   :param arrow_length: Length of the arrow head (px).
   :type arrow_length: float, default=0.25
   :param arrow_size: Size of the arrow.
   :type arrow_size: float, default=1
   :param arrow_color: Color of the arrows.
                       - Accepts:
                           - hex code e.g. '#f1f1f1'
                           - color name (of a limited set of colors).
                           - RGB/RGBA e.g. 'rgb(0, 0, 255)', 'rgba(0, 0, 255, 0.5)'.
                       - Applies to continuous (non-categorical) data.
   :type arrow_color: str, default='#3f3f3f'
   :param arrow_angle: Angle of the arrow head in degrees.
   :type arrow_angle: float, default=10
   :param legend_ondata: whether to show legend on data
   :type legend_ondata: bool, default=False
   :param ondata_size: size of the legend (text) on data.
   :type ondata_size: float, default=12
   :param ondata_color: color of the legend (text) on data
   :type ondata_color: str, default='#3f3f3f'
   :param ondata_fontface: fontface of the legend (text) on data.
                           https://lets-plot.org/python/pages/aesthetics.html#font-face
   :type ondata_fontface: str, default='bold'
   :param ondata_family: family of the legend (text) on data.
                         https://lets-plot.org/python/pages/aesthetics.html#font-family
   :type ondata_family: str, default='sans'
   :param ondata_alpha: alpha (transparency) of the legend on data.
   :type ondata_alpha: float, default=1
   :param ondata_weighted: whether to use weighted mean for the legend on data.
                           If True, the weighted mean of the group means is used.
                           If False, the arithmetic mean of the group means is used.
   :type ondata_weighted: bool, default=True
   :param \*\*point_kwargs: Additional parameters for the `geom_point` layer.
                            For more information on geom_point parameters, see:
                            https://lets-plot.org/python/pages/api/lets_plot.geom_point.html

   :returns: Dimensional reduction plot.
   :rtype: PlotSpec


.. py:function:: dimensionals(data: anndata.AnnData, keys: collections.abc.Sequence[str], *, mapping: lets_plot.plot.core.FeatureSpec | None = None, dimensions: Literal['umap', 'pca', 'tsne'] = 'umap', use_key: str | None = None, xy: tuple[int, int] | collections.abc.Sequence[int] = (1, 2), size: float = 0.8, variable_keys: collections.abc.Sequence[str] | str | None = None, tooltips: Literal['none'] | collections.abc.Sequence[str] | lets_plot.plot.core.FeatureSpec | None = None, interactive: bool = False, observations_name: str = 'Barcode', color_low: str = '#e6e6e6', color_mid: str | None = None, color_high: str = '#377eb8', mid_point: Literal['mean', 'median', 'mid'] | float = 'median', axis_type: Literal['axis', 'arrow'] | None = None, arrow_length: float = 0.25, arrow_size: float = 1, arrow_color: str = '#3f3f3f', arrow_angle: float = 10, legend_ondata: bool = False, ondata_size: float = 12, ondata_color: str = '#3f3f3f', ondata_fontface: str = 'bold', ondata_family: str = 'sans', ondata_alpha: float = 1, ondata_weighted: bool = True, share_labels: bool = True, share_axis: bool = False, layers: collections.abc.Sequence[lets_plot.plot.core.FeatureSpec | lets_plot.plot.core.LayerSpec] | lets_plot.plot.core.FeatureSpec | lets_plot.plot.core.LayerSpec | None = None, ncol: int | None = None, sharex: str | None = None, sharey: str | None = None, widths: list[float] | None = None, heights: list[float] | None = None, hspace: float | None = None, vspace: float | None = None, fit: bool | None = None, align: bool | None = None, guides: str = 'auto', **point_kwargs) -> lets_plot.plot.subplots.SupPlotsSpec

   Grid of dimensionality reduction plots.

   :param data: The AnnData object of the single cell data.
   :type data: AnnData
   :param keys: The keys (cell features) to color the points by.
                e.g., 'leiden' or 'louvain' to color by clusters or gene name for expression.
   :type keys: list[str] | tuple[str] | Sequence[str]
   :param mapping: Additional aesthetic mappings for the plot, the result of `aes()`.
   :type mapping: FeatureSpec | None, default=None
   :param dimensions: The dimensional reduction method to use.
                      e.g., 'umap' or 'pca' or 'tsne'.
   :type dimensions: Literal['umap', 'pca', 'tsne'], default='umap'
   :param xy: The x and y axes to use for the plot.
              e.g., (1, 2) for UMAP1 and UMAP2.
   :type xy: tuple[int, int] | Sequence[int], default=(1, 2)
   :param use_key: The specific key to use for the desired dimensions.
                   e.g., 'X_umap_2d' or 'X_pca_2d'.
                   Otherwise, the function will decide on the key based on the dimensions.
   :type use_key: str, default=None
   :param size: The size of the points.
   :type size: float, default=0.8
   :param variable_keys: Variable keys to add to the DataFrame. If None, no additional keys are added.
   :type variable_keys: str | Sequence[str] | None, default=None
   :param tooltips: Tooltips to show when hovering over the geom.
                    Accepts Sequence[str] or result of `layer_tooltips()` for more complex tooltips.
                    Use 'none' to disable tooltips.
   :type tooltips: Literal['none'] | Sequence[str] | FeatureSpec | None, default=None
   :param interactive: Whether to make the plot interactive.
   :type interactive: bool, default=False
   :param cluster_name: The name to overwrite the clustering key in the dataframe and the plot.
   :type cluster_name: str, default='Cluster'
   :param observations_name: The name to give to barcode (or index) column in the dataframe.
   :type observations_name: str, default='Barcode'
   :param color_low: The color to use for the low end of the color gradient.
                     - Accepts:
                         - hex code e.g. '#f1f1f1'
                         - color name (of a limited set of colors).
                         - RGB/RGBA e.g. 'rgb(0, 0, 255)', 'rgba(0, 0, 255, 0.5)'.
                     - Applies to continuous (non-categorical) data.
   :type color_low: str, default='#e6e6e6'
   :param color_mid: The color to use for the middle part of the color gradient.
                     - Accepts:
                         - hex code e.g. '#f1f1f1'
                         - color name (of a limited set of colors).
                         - RGB/RGBA e.g. 'rgb(0, 0, 255)', 'rgba(0, 0, 255, 0.5)'.
                     - Applies to continuous (non-categorical) data.
   :type color_mid: str, default=None
   :param color_high: The color to use for the high end of the color gradient.
                      - Accepts:
                          - hex code e.g. '#f1f1f1'
                          - color name (of a limited set of colors).
                          - RGB/RGBA e.g. 'rgb(0, 0, 255)', 'rgba(0, 0, 255, 0.5)'.
                      - Applies to continuous (non-categorical) data.
   :type color_high: str, default='#377EB8'
   :param mid_point: The midpoint (in data value) of the color gradient.
                     Can be 'mean', 'median' and 'mid' or a number (float or int).
                     - If 'mean', the midpoint is the mean of the data.
                     - If 'median', the midpoint is the median of the data.
                     - If 'mid', the midpoint is the mean of 'min' and 'max' of the data.
   :type mid_point: Literal["mean", "median", "mid"] | float, default="median"
   :param axis_type: Whether to use regular axis or arrows as the axis.
   :type axis_type: Literal["axis", "arrow"] | None
   :param arrow_length: Length of the arrow head (px).
   :type arrow_length: float, default=0.25
   :param arrow_size: Size of the arrow.
   :type arrow_size: float, default=1
   :param arrow_color: Color of the arrows.
                       - Accepts:
                           - hex code e.g. '#f1f1f1'
                           - color name (of a limited set of colors).
                           - RGB/RGBA e.g. 'rgb(0, 0, 255)', 'rgba(0, 0, 255, 0.5)'.
                       - Applies to continuous (non-categorical) data.
   :type arrow_color: str, default='#3f3f3f'
   :param arrow_angle: Angle of the arrow head in degrees.
   :type arrow_angle: float, default=10
   :param legend_ondata: whether to show legend on data
   :type legend_ondata: bool, default=False
   :param ondata_size: size of the legend (text) on data.
   :type ondata_size: float, default=12
   :param ondata_color: color of the legend (text) on data
   :type ondata_color: str, default='#3f3f3f'
   :param ondata_fontface: fontface of the legend (text) on data.
                           https://lets-plot.org/python/pages/aesthetics.html#font-face
   :type ondata_fontface: str, default='bold'
   :param ondata_family: family of the legend (text) on data.
                         https://lets-plot.org/python/pages/aesthetics.html#font-family
   :type ondata_family: str, default='sans'
   :param ondata_alpha: alpha (transparency) of the legend on data.
   :type ondata_alpha: float, default=1
   :param ondata_weighted: whether to use weighted mean for the legend on data.
                           If True, the weighted mean of the group means is used.
                           If False, the arithmetic mean of the group means is used.
   :type ondata_weighted: bool, default=True
   :param share_labels: Whether to share the labels across all plots.
                        If True, only X labels on bottom row and Y labels on left column are shown.
   :type share_labels: bool, default=True
   :param share_axis: Whether to share the axis across all plots.
                      If True, only X axis on bottom row and Y axis on left column is shown.
   :type share_axis: bool, default=False
   :param layers: Layers to add to all the plots in the grid.
   :type layers: Sequence[FeatureSpec|LayerSpec] | FeatureSpec | LayerSpec | None, default=None,
   :param ncol: Number of columns in grid. If not specified, shows plots horizontally, in one row.
   :type ncol: int, default=None
   :param sharex: Controls sharing of axis limits between subplots in the grid.
                  `all`/True - share limits between all subplots.
                  `none`/False - do not share limits between subplots.
                  `row` - share limits between subplots in the same row.
                  `col` - share limits between subplots in the same column.
   :type sharex: bool, default=None
   :param sharey: Controls sharing of axis limits between subplots in the grid.
                  `all`/True - share limits between all subplots.
                  `none`/False - do not share limits between subplots.
                  `row` - share limits between subplots in the same row.
                  `col` - share limits between subplots in the same column.
   :type sharey: bool, default=None
   :param widths: Relative width of each column of grid, left to right.
   :type widths: list[float], default=None
   :param heights: Relative height of each row of grid, top-down.
   :type heights: list[float], default=None
   :param hspace: Cell horizontal spacing in px.
   :type hspace: float | None = None
   :param vspace: Cell vertical spacing in px.
   :type vspace: float | None = None
   :param fit: Whether to stretch each plot to match the aspect ratio of its cell (fit=True),
               or to preserve the original aspect ratio of plots (fit=False).
   :type fit: bool, default=True
   :param align: If True, align inner areas (i.e. “geom” bounds) of plots.
                 However, cells containing other (sub)grids are not participating
                 in the plot “inner areas” layouting.
   :type align: bool, default=False
   :param guides: Specifies how guides (legends and colorbars) should be treated in the layout.
                  - 'collect'- collect guides from all subplots, removing duplicates.
                  - 'keep' - keep guides in their original subplots; do not collect at this level.
                  - 'auto' - allow guides to be collected if an upper-level layout uses guides='collect';
                  otherwise, keep them in subplots.

                  Duplicates are identified by comparing visual properties:
                  For legends: title, labels, and all aesthetic values (colors, shapes, sizes, etc.).
                  For colorbars: title, domain limits, breaks, and color gradient.
   :type guides: str, default="auto"
   :param For more information on gggrid parameters:
   :param https:
   :type https: //lets-plot.org/python/pages/api/lets_plot.gggrid.html
   :param \*\*point_kwargs: Additional parameters for the `geom_point` layer.
                            For more information on geom_point parameters, see:
                            https://lets-plot.org/python/pages/api/lets_plot.geom_point.html

   :returns: Grid of dimensionality reduction plots.
   :rtype: SupPlotsSpec


.. py:function:: expressions(data: anndata.AnnData, keys: collections.abc.Sequence[str], *, mapping: lets_plot.plot.core.FeatureSpec | None = None, dimensions: Literal['umap', 'pca', 'tsne'] = 'umap', use_key: str | None = None, xy: tuple[int, int] | collections.abc.Sequence[int] = (1, 2), size: float = 0.8, variable_keys: collections.abc.Sequence[str] | str | None = None, tooltips: Literal['none'] | collections.abc.Sequence[str] | lets_plot.plot.core.FeatureSpec | None = None, interactive: bool = False, observations_name: str = 'Barcode', color_low: str = '#e6e6e6', color_mid: str | None = None, color_high: str = '#377eb8', mid_point: Literal['mean', 'median', 'mid'] | float = 'median', axis_type: Literal['axis', 'arrow'] | None = None, arrow_length: float = 0.25, arrow_size: float = 1, arrow_color: str = '#3f3f3f', arrow_angle: float = 10, legend_ondata: bool = False, ondata_size: float = 12, ondata_color: str = '#3f3f3f', ondata_fontface: str = 'bold', ondata_family: str = 'sans', ondata_alpha: float = 1, ondata_weighted: bool = True, share_labels: bool = True, share_axis: bool = False, layers: collections.abc.Sequence[lets_plot.plot.core.FeatureSpec | lets_plot.plot.core.LayerSpec] | lets_plot.plot.core.FeatureSpec | lets_plot.plot.core.LayerSpec | None = None, ncol: int | None = None, sharex: str | None = None, sharey: str | None = None, widths: list[float] | None = None, heights: list[float] | None = None, hspace: float | None = None, vspace: float | None = None, fit: bool | None = None, align: bool | None = None, guides: str = 'auto', **point_kwargs) -> lets_plot.plot.subplots.SupPlotsSpec

   Grid of dimensionality reduction plots.

   :param data: The AnnData object of the single cell data.
   :type data: AnnData
   :param keys: The keys (gene names) to color the points by.
   :type keys: list[str] | tuple[str] | Sequence[str]
   :param mapping: Additional aesthetic mappings for the plot, the result of `aes()`.
   :type mapping: FeatureSpec | None, default=None
   :param dimensions: The dimensional reduction method to use.
                      e.g., 'umap' or 'pca' or 'tsne'.
   :type dimensions: Literal['umap', 'pca', 'tsne'], default='umap'
   :param xy: The x and y axes to use for the plot.
              e.g., (1, 2) for UMAP1 and UMAP2.
   :type xy: tuple[int, int] | Sequence[int], default=(1, 2)
   :param use_key: The specific key to use for the desired dimensions.
                   e.g., 'X_umap_2d' or 'X_pca_2d'.
                   Otherwise, the function will decide on the key based on the dimensions.
   :type use_key: str, default=None
   :param size: The size of the points.
   :type size: float, default=0.8
   :param variable_keys: Variable keys to add to the DataFrame. If None, no additional keys are added.
   :type variable_keys: str | Sequence[str] | None, default=None
   :param tooltips: Tooltips to show when hovering over the geom.
                    Accepts Sequence[str] or result of `layer_tooltips()` for more complex tooltips.
                    Use 'none' to disable tooltips.
   :type tooltips: Literal['none'] | Sequence[str] | FeatureSpec | None, default=None
   :param interactive: Whether to make the plot interactive.
   :type interactive: bool, default=False
   :param cluster_name: The name to overwrite the clustering key in the dataframe and the plot.
   :type cluster_name: str, default='Cluster'
   :param observations_name: The name to give to barcode (or index) column in the dataframe.
   :type observations_name: str, default='Barcode'
   :param color_low: The color to use for the low end of the color gradient.
                     - Accepts:
                         - hex code e.g. '#f1f1f1'
                         - color name (of a limited set of colors).
                         - RGB/RGBA e.g. 'rgb(0, 0, 255)', 'rgba(0, 0, 255, 0.5)'.
                     - Applies to continuous (non-categorical) data.
   :type color_low: str, default='#e6e6e6'
   :param color_mid: The color to use for the middle part of the color gradient.
                     - Accepts:
                         - hex code e.g. '#f1f1f1'
                         - color name (of a limited set of colors).
                         - RGB/RGBA e.g. 'rgb(0, 0, 255)', 'rgba(0, 0, 255, 0.5)'.
                     - Applies to continuous (non-categorical) data.
   :type color_mid: str, default=None
   :param color_high: The color to use for the high end of the color gradient.
                      - Accepts:
                          - hex code e.g. '#f1f1f1'
                          - color name (of a limited set of colors).
                          - RGB/RGBA e.g. 'rgb(0, 0, 255)', 'rgba(0, 0, 255, 0.5)'.
                      - Applies to continuous (non-categorical) data.
   :type color_high: str, default='#377EB8'
   :param mid_point: The midpoint (in data value) of the color gradient.
                     Can be 'mean', 'median' and 'mid' or a number (float or int).
                     - If 'mean', the midpoint is the mean of the data.
                     - If 'median', the midpoint is the median of the data.
                     - If 'mid', the midpoint is the mean of 'min' and 'max' of the data.
   :type mid_point: Literal["mean", "median", "mid"] | float, default="median"
   :param axis_type: Whether to use regular axis or arrows as the axis.
   :type axis_type: Literal["axis", "arrow"] | None
   :param arrow_length: Length of the arrow head (px).
   :type arrow_length: float, default=0.25
   :param arrow_size: Size of the arrow.
   :type arrow_size: float, default=1
   :param arrow_color: Color of the arrows.
                       - Accepts:
                           - hex code e.g. '#f1f1f1'
                           - color name (of a limited set of colors).
                           - RGB/RGBA e.g. 'rgb(0, 0, 255)', 'rgba(0, 0, 255, 0.5)'.
                       - Applies to continuous (non-categorical) data.
   :type arrow_color: str, default='#3f3f3f'
   :param arrow_angle: Angle of the arrow head in degrees.
   :type arrow_angle: float, default=10
   :param legend_ondata: whether to show legend on data
   :type legend_ondata: bool, default=False
   :param ondata_size: size of the legend (text) on data.
   :type ondata_size: float, default=12
   :param ondata_color: color of the legend (text) on data
   :type ondata_color: str, default='#3f3f3f'
   :param ondata_fontface: fontface of the legend (text) on data.
                           https://lets-plot.org/python/pages/aesthetics.html#font-face
   :type ondata_fontface: str, default='bold'
   :param ondata_family: family of the legend (text) on data.
                         https://lets-plot.org/python/pages/aesthetics.html#font-family
   :type ondata_family: str, default='sans'
   :param ondata_alpha: alpha (transparency) of the legend on data.
   :type ondata_alpha: float, default=1
   :param ondata_weighted: whether to use weighted mean for the legend on data.
                           If True, the weighted mean of the group means is used.
                           If False, the arithmetic mean of the group means is used.
   :type ondata_weighted: bool, default=True
   :param share_labels: Whether to share the labels across all plots.
                        If True, only X labels on bottom row and Y labels on left column are shown.
   :type share_labels: bool, default=True
   :param share_axis: Whether to share the axis across all plots.
                      If True, only X axis on bottom row and Y axis on left column is shown.
   :type share_axis: bool, default=False
   :param layers: Layers to add to all the plots in the grid.
   :type layers: Sequence[FeatureSpec|LayerSpec] | FeatureSpec | LayerSpec | None, default=None,
   :param ncol: Number of columns in grid. If not specified, shows plots horizontally, in one row.
   :type ncol: int, default=None
   :param sharex: Controls sharing of axis limits between subplots in the grid.
                  `all`/True - share limits between all subplots.
                  `none`/False - do not share limits between subplots.
                  `row` - share limits between subplots in the same row.
                  `col` - share limits between subplots in the same column.
   :type sharex: bool, default=None
   :param sharey: Controls sharing of axis limits between subplots in the grid.
